$comment: Pinned, SHA-verified V8 prebuilt channel for the in-process JS host (src/runtime/js/, issue #76 / L-61 / R-LANG-002/008 / R-SEC-009). SINGLE source of truth for the pins — parsed by tools/fetch_v8.py (download + verify-before-use + decompress). src/runtime/js/CMakeLists.txt forwards this file's PATH to fetch_v8.py (it does NOT parse the pins itself, so they are never duplicated) and derives the extracted lib filename (rusty_v8.lib / librusty_v8.a) by the platform naming convention. Channel = denoland/rusty_v8 SHA-pinned third-party prebuilt (the wgpu-native / CEF precedent in docs/signing.md: third-party build libs are authenticated by their OWN publisher via TLS + SHA-pin + verify-before-use, and are explicitly OUT of scope for the engine R-SEC-009 first-party trust root — do NOT route these through tools/verify_artifact.py). rusty_v8 ships no C++ headers, so the coherent V8 C++ headers come from the version-locked v8-<rusty_v8_version>.crate on crates.io (carries v8/include/, incl. v8-inspector.h + cppgc/). VERSION-COHERENCE (rusty_v8_version == the header crate version) is THE critical correctness invariant — fetch_v8.py asserts it at runtime (fail-closed) and tools/tests/test_fetch_v8.py asserts it statically. Migrating to an owned-from-source vcpkg V8 port is a SEPARATE pre-1.0 hardening task (see src/runtime/js/README.md § From-source migration seam).
